waves.cadence: continuous until top 200 held hypotheses and 79 control policies are coded
waves.objective: Backfill exact event dates, treated units, comparison units, exemptions, thresholds, and phase-ins for the highest-priority held hypotheses and all control-focused policies.

waves.cadence: weekly access and metadata loop, with public aggregate pulls whenever available
waves.objective: Build a household incidence spine from public or research-access household, tax-benefit, consumption, and distributional sources.

waves.cadence: daily until public QCEW, LEHD/QWI, and ILOSTAT manifests are in place
waves.objective: Pull public labor/payroll panels and define restricted linked-employer-employee access targets.
Create a reusable welfare-accounting ledger schema and populate first public case rows from treatment, household, labor, fiscal, price, quality, and externality panels.

waves.cadence: daily for ready trade panels, weekly for procurement and firm microdata
waves.objective: Use ready trade sources first, then normalize procurement and firm dynamics panels for industrial policy, trade, competition, and implementation-capacity tests.

waves.cadence: daily for EIA public data, weekly scout loop for ENTSO-E and regulator portals
waves.objective: Pull energy reliability, outage, fuel-stock, and price panels for energy price controls, subsidies, and transition-policy second-order tests.

waves.cadence: every 2 hours through the existing city-level hunt loop
waves.objective: Extend the existing city-level sprint into second-order gate promotion by joining regulated/exempt treatment coding to rent, permits, quality, eviction, listing, and mobility layers.

waves.cadence: weekly source discovery and access loop
waves.objective: Build price-control scarcity layers from official price schedules, scanner access targets, stockout/complaint sources, and parallel-market price panels.
